what_it_does: This file is a metadata record for a channel attachment: it declares an attachment id, channel, attachment type, target image URI, and creation timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter only; no body content
added timestamp is precise to microseconds with timezone
standouts: target URI points to an icon named 'Feature_2_ChangeStyle' under design-assets
file contains no prose or embedded image data
it is a ledger-style attachment record in the design-assets channel
conventions: Uses id, channel, attachment_type, target, added fields
concerns: target URI scheme 'image://' is not a standard filesystem or HTTP reference
no alt text, description, or content hash for the image
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that maps att-e198a593 to an icon asset in the design-assets channel.
questions: How is the 'image://' URI resolved by the consuming system?
Is this attachment referenced by a specific channel message or design document?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file's purpose is to record attachment metadata, and it does exactly that.
intention_signals: Register a specific design-assets image icon under a stable attachment id so it can be referenced independently of its storage location.
novelty: Use of a custom 'image://' URI scheme within a channel-memory attachment ledger.
contribution: Provides a stable, timestamped attachment id and a target mapping for an image asset in the design-assets channel.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment ledger entry mapping att-e198a593 to the image URI image://design-assets/icons/Feature_2_ChangeStyle in the design-assets channel.
intention_for_embedding: Record and expose an image asset attachment for the design-assets channel.
